sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Gift of 10,000 SS Innovations (SSII) shares by affiliated entity
feed.summary: SS Innovations International, Inc.'s Chairman and CEO, Sudhir Srivastava, reported an indirect transfer of 10,000 common stock shares as a bona fide gift. The shares were held by Sushruta Pvt. Ltd., an entity in which Srivastava holds a controlling interest. Following this transaction, entities associated with Srivastava indirectly hold 109,585,767 shares of SS Innovations common stock, with the transfer classified as a routine personal transfer rather than a market sale.

feed.title: DA Davidson Reiterates Buy Rating on Tractor Supply Company (TSCO)
feed.summary: DA Davidson analyst Michael Baker has reiterated a Buy rating and a $60.00 price target for Tractor Supply Company (TSCO), despite reducing his first-quarter comparable store sales estimate. The analyst anticipates TSCO will reaffirm its full-year guidance, emphasizing that commentary on sales pace and commodity inflation will be key drivers for the stock. The reiteration is based on the stock's year-to-date underperformance, which has brought its relative multiple to its lowest level since 2018.

feed.title: Stellantis set to hire Hyundai executive for key North America role
feed.summary: Stellantis is reportedly hiring Michael Orange, a long-serving Hyundai executive, for a senior North American role under CEO Antonio Filosa. This strategic move aims to invigorate Stellantis's U.S. sales, which have lagged behind competitors like Hyundai. Orange is credited with significantly boosting Hyundai's sales during his tenure, making him a valuable recruit for Stellantis's turnaround efforts.

feed.title: News Corp (NASDAQ: NWSA) details $1B buy-back and ASX notices
feed.summary: News Corp has reported ongoing activity under its $1 billion stock repurchase program for Nasdaq-listed Class A and Class B common stock. The company has provided daily buy-back notifications to the Australian Securities Exchange (ASX), detailing millions of shares repurchased for cash. Goldman Sachs & Co. LLC is acting as the broker for these transactions, and the program aims to enhance shareholder value.

feed.title: PepsiCo tops quarterly revenue estimates as price cuts drive demand
feed.summary: PepsiCo (PEP.O) exceeded first-quarter revenue estimates, reporting $19.44 billion against an estimated $18.94 billion, and maintained its full-year targets. This performance was attributed to recent price reductions for snack brands which boosted demand in the U.S., alongside strong sales in energy drinks and prebiotic sodas. The company also announced a restage of Gatorade brands with new low-sugar formulas to further drive growth.

feed.title: PepsiCo's Q1 revenue up 8.5% to $19.4B
feed.summary: PepsiCo reported an 8.5% increase in its first-quarter revenue, reaching $19.4 billion. This indicates a strong financial performance for the beverage and snack giant.

feed.title: BTIG initiates Henry Schein stock coverage with Neutral on modest growth
feed.summary: BTIG has initiated coverage on Henry Schein Inc. (NASDAQ:HSIC) with a Neutral rating, citing the company's market leadership but modest growth rates despite its significant presence in dental and medical markets. The firm notes that Henry Schein's growth is constrained by a competitive market, particularly on the medical distribution side. While anticipating some growth re-acceleration in 2026, BTIG views the business as mature with low-to-mid single-digit growth and considers the stock overvalued based on its current P/E ratio.

feed.title: Gallagher expands UK real estate insurance with Bridge deal
feed.summary: Arthur J. Gallagher & Co. (NYSE:AJG) has acquired UK-based Bridge Insurance Brokers Limited, a commercial broker specializing in real estate, construction, corporate, and private client insurance. The acquisition aims to strengthen Gallagher's UK retail operations and expand its expertise in real estate and construction, with Bridge's existing leadership continuing to operate from Manchester and London. Financial terms of the transaction were not disclosed.

feed.title: Spotify Technology Is One of Analyst’s Favourite, Here Is Why?
feed.summary: Spotify Technology S.A. (NYSE: SPOT) is highlighted as a favored stock by analysts, maintaining its dominant position in the music streaming market with a global subscriber share of 31% to 32.9%. Analysts like Daiwa's Jonathan Kees have initiated coverage with an "Outperform" rating and a price target of $535, citing stable subscriber additions, improving ad revenue, price increases, and expansion into audiobooks and podcasts as growth drivers. The company also increased its investment in Australia's music ecosystem, paying over AUD $330 million to local rightsholders in 2025.

feed.title: The cheapest bitcoin ETF yet: Morgan Stanley uses 0.14% fee to draw $100 million in first week
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley's new MSBT spot bitcoin ETF has quickly attracted over $100 million in its first week due to its market-low 0.14% expense ratio and the firm's extensive wealth management network. This successful launch highlights increasing competition among major financial institutions, with rivals like Goldman Sachs and BlackRock also developing new, more structured bitcoin investment products beyond simple spot exposure. The entry of traditional Wall Street firms signals a broader shift in how investors are accessing bitcoin.
